title: FileZilla Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for using FileZilla effectively.

commands.scenario: Use when you need to start the FileZilla application to manage FTP connections.

commands.description: Connect to an SFTP server using FileZilla
commands.scenario: Use when you need to securely transfer files over SFTP using FileZilla.

commands.description: Connect to an FTP server using FileZilla
commands.scenario: Use when you need to transfer files using FTP protocol in FileZilla.

commands.command: Edit -> Settings -> Transfers -> Maximum simultaneous transfers
commands.description: Set the maximum number of simultaneous file transfers
commands.scenario: Use when you need to optimize transfer speed by adjusting simultaneous transfers.
Use to prevent disconnections by adjusting timeout settings in FileZilla.

commands.description: Create a new site entry in Site Manager
commands.scenario: Use when you need to save connection details for frequent access.

commands.description: Change the language of the FileZilla interface
commands.scenario: Use when you need to switch the interface language for better usability.
Use when you need to compare local and remote directories for differences.

commands.description: Apply filename filters to hide/show files
commands.scenario: Use when you need to filter files by name to simplify file management.

commands.description: Set default file types for ASCII/Binary transfer
commands.scenario: Use to ensure correct file type handling during transfers.
Use to ensure you are running the latest version of FileZilla.

commands.description: Set speed limits for file transfers
commands.scenario: Use when you need to control bandwidth usage during transfers.

commands.description: Export FileZilla settings to a file
